Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Attend A Psychiatric Evalaution
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Off Calendar

DEFENDANT INFORMATICA LLC AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND A PSYCHIATRIC EVALAUTION.
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TARA H. HATTENDORF, Esq. of Marlis Park, P.C. (Tel: 323-922-2000) for plaintiff;
Atty. CONNIE L. MICHAELS, Esq. of Littler Mendelson P.C. (Tel: 310-553-0308) for defendant Informatica LLC and Ben Kennedy.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT INFORMATICA LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO ATTEND A PSYCHIATRIC EVALAUTION.

Natter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

This motion is denied without prejudice to be renewed before the trial judge. The trial date is August 1, 2022. Given plaintiff's request that the psychiatric evaluation not take place for "60 days after the June 29, 2022, letter," the parties need to coordinate the evaluation with the trial judge.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anita Alderson, CSR No. 11843, Email: anita.alderson@ccrola.com, Tel: 213-471-2966 (302/RBU)

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS BEN KENNEDY AND INFORMATICA LLC'S MOTION FOR CASE TERMINATING SANCTIONS OR LESSER SANCTIONS FOR SPOLIATION OF EVIDENCE BY PLAINTIFF STEPHEN DEUXIER:

The motion for terminating sanctions or lesser sanctions for spoliation of evidence is granted in part and otherwise denied. Plaintiff shall not offer in evidence any part of the memorandum of his telephone conversation with Ben Kennedy dated August 16, 2019, which does not appear in his email to Amy Maxwell dated August 20, 2019. This ruling constitutes a sanction for discovery abuse in the plaintiff's failure to make a more timely production of the surviving copy of the memorandum dated August 16, 2019. The motion is otherwise denied.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Gail Peebles, CSR# 11458 (213.219.8455; gailpeebles@sbcglobal.net). =(302/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BEN KENNEDY and INFORMATICA LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT:

Defendants' motion to strike paragraphs 69 and 70 is denied. The extent of defendants' alleged publication/s raises a factual issue that cannot be resolved on this non-speaking motion. Plaintiff's allegations that the defendants repeated the defamation on more than one occasion is not false, improper, or irrelevant under Code of Civil Procedure section 436(a). The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to paragraph 71. The allegations of future republications, which may never occur, is improper. (See Dible v. Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. (2009) 170 Cal. App. 4th 842, 851 ["A defamation action does not exist, of course, for a statement that one might make in the future." (emphasis in original)].)

One order signed for demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BEN KENNEDY and INFORMATICA LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:

Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for defamation (libel and slander) in the complaint is overruled. The Court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 452. Plaintiff alleges that defendants made false statements of fact regarding plaintiff's job performance in its termination memorandum and subsequently in writing and orally. (Complaint, pars. 35-43, 68-77.) Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the substance of the defamation. (See Okun v. Superior Court (1981) 29 Cal.3d 442, 458 ["[S]lander can be charged by alleging the substance of the defamatory statement."] Moreover, defendants should be aware of the allegedly false statements that were in its termination letter. (See Okun, 29 Cal.3d at 458 ["Less particularity is required when it appears that defendant has superior knowledge of the facts, so long as the pleading gives notice of the issues sufficient to enable preparation of a defense."]; Ludgate Insurance Co. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 592, 608 [" 'The particularity required in pleading facts depends on the extent to which the defendant in fairness needs detailed information that can be conveniently provided by the plaintiff; less particularity is required where the defendant may be assumed to have knowledge of the facts equal to that possessed by the plaintiff.' [Citation.]" There is no need to require specificity in the pleadings because 'modern discovery procedures necessarily affect the amount of detail that should be required in a pleading.'"].) Plaintiff alleges that the defamation was published to third parties and the extent of the publication/s is a factual issue for discovery. (See, e.g., Complaint, par. 69.) Plaintiff also sufficiently pleads damages as he alleges defamation per se regarding his competency to perform his vocation and damages are presumed. (See Civ. Code sec. 46; Contento v. Mitchell (1972) 28 Cal.App.3d 356, 358 [damage to reputation presumed based on defamation per se].) Lastly, plaintiff sufficiently alleges malice to avoid the common interest privilege. The malice can be inferred from plaintiff's allegations that defendants acted with discriminatory and retaliatory animus. (See Complaint, pars. 65, 75; Kelly v. General Telephone Co. (1982) 136 Cal.App.3d 278, 284 [allegations of retaliatory motive support allegation of malice to defeat qualified privilege on demurrer].)

One order signed for demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
